rulings.Case Title: PACIFIC PEJIU WU RESTAURANT PARTNERS L.P. VS. JAMES HARAMIS
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, AUGUST 22, 2007, LINE 1. CROSS-COMPLAINANT JAMES HARAMIS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS CONTINUED TO OCTOBER 11, 2007, ON THE COURT'S OWN MOTION. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT AREVALO et al VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, AUGUST 22, 2007, LINE 2. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS Granted. Plaintiffs are unable to establish a prima facie case of race or national origin or gender discrimination. Both white male and female employees were laid off as a part of the City's budget problems. Plaintiffs were placed in other jobs in the City as were the two female employees about whom plaintiffs complain. Plaintiff contends that the females received a better job, but it was one without benefits and it was one that the females had prior experience doing. The decision to terminate plaintiffs was admittedly made by the head of the department, Ed Lee, and there is no evidence that his decision was based on race or sex; rather, it was based on the need to cut the budget. As a consequence, the derogatory comments of Montgomery and Monahan do not help Plaintiffs establish a prima facie case. Assuming Plaintiffs established a prima facie case, the City has presented a legitimate, non-discriminatory reason for its actions, a budget crises, and legitimate reasons for its decision to appoint Monahan and Anderson to the street inspector position. Plaintiffs cannot demonstrate that the reasons presented were a pretext to remove Plaintiffs from their job. As to Plaintiffs' claims of retaliation, there is no evidence that Arevalo engaged in protected activity. The claims of Nguyen and Wong are barred based on their failure to exhaust administrative remedies. Wong cannot establish a causal connection between his complaints from 1998 to 2001 and the termination decision in 2005. =(302/PJM/JF)

rulings.Case Title: REPRO MEDIA, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. SOLZER & HAIL, INC. A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, AUGUST 22, 2007, LINE 6. DEFENDANTS WOLFGANG SOLZER AND SOLZER & HAIL, INC.'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND: COURT GRANTS MOTION FOR RELIEF FROM DEFAULT. DEFENDANT TO PAY $1,500.00 AS REASONABLE FEES APPLICABLE TO PLAINTIFF OBTAINING DEFAULT. GRANT APPLICATION TO FILE AMENDED ANSWER AND CROSS-COMPLAINT. CERTAIN OF THE CLAIMS ARE COMPULSORY AND C.C.P. SECTION 426.50 FAVORS GRANTING AMENDMENTS WHERE THE CLAIMS ARE COMPULSORY. GIVEN THAT, IT MAKES SENSE TO RESOLVE ALL OF THE PARTIES' DISPUTES AT ONE TIME. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: ABT LLC A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY VS. JOSE L MORALES et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, AUGUST 22, 2007, LINE 14. PLAINTIFF ABT, L.L.C.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT: Hearing required to consider defendant's evidence as to plaintiff's intent. Motion for summary adjudication is granted as to the following defenses: 2nd, discrimination because defendant has not presented any triable issues of fact; 4th breach of implied warranty of habitability and 5th cause of action, breach of covenant; based on Drouet v. Superior Court (2003) 31 Cal.4th 583, 599. 3rd, noncompliance with the rent ordinance, 6th, noncompliance with the Ellis Act and 7th notice to withdraw as a violation of the Rent Ordinance. Each of these defenses is based on the same facts - the statement that the rent was $873.72 when it is undisputed that the rent was $862.41 and the failure of the notice to refer to unit 572A. The disputed facts are not material. The defendant knew the correct amount of the rent and the purpose of the notice was not to require the defendant to pay the sum of $873.72; it was to advise the tenant that the property was going to be taken off the rental market. There also is no claim in this case for rent. It is undisputed that the defendant did not occupy 572A and that he knew the unit was vacant. There is no showing that whatever confusion arguably exists as to the numbering of the units that the tenant was in anyway confused or mislead as to the intent of the landlord. It also appears that the defendant, by accepting the benefits of the Ellis Act, waived whatever right the tenant arguably had to defeat the landlord's claim on these grounds. =(302/PJM/AP)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT BARBI VS. NATHAN WEINSTEIN et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, AUGUST 22, 2007, LINE 12. DEFENDANT NATHAN WEINSTEIN'S DEMURRER TO THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED, FIVE DAYS TO ANSWER. THERE IS NO REALISTIC RISK TO DEFENDANT OF MULTIPLE CLAIMS BEING FILED AND RELIEF CAN BE GRANTED BASED ON THE PARTIES WHO ARE BEFORE THE COURT. =(302/PJM/PB)
